title: Convolution of Causal Signals
summary: This page presents Example 3.20, computing the convolution of two causal discrete-time exponentials x[n]=(0.8)^n u[n] and g[n]=(0.3)^n u[n]. It uses causality to limit the convolution sum, evaluates the resulting geometric series, and obtains c[n] = 2[(0.8)^{n+1} - (0.3)^{n+1}]u[n], followed by a related drill problem.
